req.title: Recurring transfer
req.statement: A premium-tier customer shall be able to schedule a recurring transfer up to $10,000 per occurrence; non-premium accounts capped at $2,500.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines two equivalence partitions for customer tier (standard vs premium), each with distinct per-occurrence caps ($2,500 and $10,000). Boundary values exist at exactly those caps and one cent above. A decision table covers the cross-product of tier x amount x frequency. Negative paths cover limit exceeded and unsupported frequency. State transition covers the lifecycle: scheduled -> next_run calculated. Tests are ordered: (1) positive EP for premium within cap, (2) negative NEG for standard exceeding cap, (3) boundary BVA at premium cap exactly, (4) negative NEG for unsupported frequency, (5) DT cross-tier decision, (6) STC confirming next_run state after scheduling.

tests.name: test_premium_recurring_transfer_within_limit
actions: Authenticate as premium customer via <fixture: auth_headers_premium>
expected: Response body contains next_run as a valid ISO-8601 date
Response body echoes amount=9000.00 and frequency=monthly
trace: R-FIN-005: next run calculated => next_run present in response
tests.executable:
import pytest
from dateutil.parser import parse as parse_dt

def test_premium_recurring_transfer_within_limit(api_client, auth_headers_premium):
    payload = {
        "amount": 9000.00,
        "frequency": "monthly",
        "source_account": "<fixture: premium_account_id>",
        "destination_account": "<fixture: dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=auth_headers_premium)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in body
    assert body["amount"] == 9000.00
    assert body["frequency"] == "monthly"
    next_run = body.get("next_run")
    assert next_run is not None
    parse_dt(next_run)  # raises if not valid ISO-8601

tests.name: test_standard_recurring_transfer_exceeds_cap_returns_422
actions: Authenticate as standard customer via <fixture: auth_headers_standard>
expected: Response body contains error code indicating per-occurrence limit exceeded
No schedule persisted in the system
trace: R-FIN-005: standard-tier cap=$2,500; 2501 > 2500 => 422 per-occurrence limit exceeded
R-FIN-005: error pathway: 422 when per-occurrence limit exceeded
tests.executable:
def test_standard_recurring_transfer_exceeds_cap_returns_422(api_client, auth_headers_standard):
    payload = {
        "amount": 2501.00,
        "frequency": "weekly",
        "source_account": "<fixture: standard_account_id>",
        "destination_account": "<fixture: dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=auth_headers_standard)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    error_detail = str(body).lower()
    assert "limit" in error_detail or "exceed" in error_detail or "cap" in error_detail

tests.executable:
def test_unsupported_frequency_returns_422(api_client, auth_headers_premium):
    payload = {
        "amount": 500.00,
        "frequency": "hourly",
        "source_account": "<fixture: premium_account_id>",
        "destination_account": "<fixture: dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=auth_headers_premium)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    error_detail = str(body).lower()
    assert "frequency" in error_detail or "unsupported" in error_detail or "invalid" in error_detail

POST /transfers/recurring with amount=2500.00, frequency=weekly (at standard cap)
expected: next_run is a valid ISO-8601 date
trace: R-FIN-005: standard-tier cap=$2,500; amount=2500.00 is inclusive boundary => 201
DT: decision table row: tier=standard, amount=cap => accept
BVA co-applied: exact boundary value for standard tier

from dateutil.parser import parse as parse_dt

def test_tier_cap_decision_table_standard_at_boundary(api_client, auth_headers_standard):
    payload = {
        "amount": 2500.00,
        "frequency": "weekly",
        "source_account": "<fixture: standard_account_id>",
        "destination_account": "<fixture: dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=auth_headers_standard)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in body
    assert body["amount"] == 2500.00
    parse_dt(body["next_run"])

tests.name: test_recurring_schedule_state_transition_next_run_populated
preconditions: No prior recurring schedule exists for this customer+destination pair
actions: Authenticate as premium customer via <fixture: auth_headers_premium>
expected: GET response next_run is a future ISO-8601 datetime
trace: R-FIN-005: schedule persisted; next run calculated
STC: state transition none->scheduled; next_run populated confirms calculation step
tests.executable:
from dateutil.parser import parse as parse_dt
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def test_recurring_schedule_state_transition_next_run_populated(api_client, auth_headers_premium):
    payload = {
        "amount": 1000.00,
        "frequency": "monthly",
        "source_account": "<fixture: premium_account_id>",
        "destination_account": "<fixture: dest_account_id>"
    }
    post_resp = api_client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=auth_headers_premium)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    schedule_id = post_resp.json()["schedule_id"]
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/transfers/recurring/{schedule_id}", headers=auth_headers_premium)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    body = get_resp.json()
    assert body.get("state") == "scheduled"
    next_run = parse_dt(body["next_run"])
    assert next_run > datetime.now(timezone.utc)
